authoritative_sources.title: Advertising and Marketing on the Internet: Rules of the Road
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator for deceptive advertising; this public guidance identifies claims, disclosures, endorsements, warranties, email, and children's issues that trigger legal, policy, or compliance review in online marketing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Campaign claims, disclosures, endorsements, warranties, email, children's issue spotting, and regulated-content escalation
used_for: claim and disclosure evidence-gap handling
legal and policy owner routing for regulated marketing content
boundary between marketing compliance support and legal interpretation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to evidence readiness, control mapping, and escalation when claims, disclosures, endorsements, or regulated content require legal or policy judgment.

authoritative_sources.title: .com Disclosures: How to Make Effective Disclosures in Digital Advertising
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC digital disclosure guidance is the primary U.S. regulator's public reference for disclosure prominence, placement, proximity, and clarity in online ads.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Digital disclosure review readiness, screenshot proof, placement evidence, and launch-block handling
used_for: disclosure checklist and screenshot evidence requirements
source conflict handling when platform or client instructions diverge from FTC disclosure expectations
refusal to approve final legal sufficiency of disclosure treatment
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires digital disclosure outputs to identify required artifacts and escalation owners while refusing legal signoff.

authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 255 - Guides Concerning the Use of Endorsements and Testimonials in Advertising
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the official public source for FTC endorsement and testimonial guides, making it authoritative for material connection, influencer, testimonial, and endorsement control mapping.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Influencer, endorsement, material-connection, testimonial, and approval evidence workflow
used_for: endorsement roster and influencer brief evidence expectations
material-connection disclosure escalation
review packet preparation for legal, editorial, or client approvers
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents influencer and testimonial handling from becoming generic content review by requiring endorsement-specific evidence and authority boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 465 - Trade Regulation Rule on the Use of Consumer Reviews and Testimonials
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the official public source for the FTC consumer reviews and testimonials rule, making it authoritative for review acquisition, display, suppression, and testimonial workflow controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Review, rating, testimonial, moderation, incentive, display, and suppression evidence workflow
used_for: review-source and testimonial evidence requirements
review suppression and incentive conflict escalation
boundary between evidence packaging and legal approval of review practices
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires review-management scenarios to preserve source records, disclosure evidence, moderation records, and owner routing instead of allowing informal approval.

authoritative_sources.title: CAN-SPAM Act: A Compliance Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC CAN-SPAM guidance is an official public business reference for commercial email requirements and directly informs evidence expectations for sender identity, subject-line support, ad identification, physical address, unsubscribe, and opt-out handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Commercial email campaign review, suppression-list, unsubscribe, and opt-out evidence workflow
used_for: email campaign evidence checklist
unsubscribe and suppression-list gap handling
escalation when email approval or legal interpretation is requested
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes email clearance dependent on traceable records and owners rather than informal campaign approval.

authoritative_sources.title: Standard Terms and Conditions for Internet Advertising for Media Buys One Year or Less, Version 3.0
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB and 4A's standard terms are widely used public contract workflow guidance for digital media buys and define insertion orders, ad materials, data use, third-party serving, delivery, measurement, discrepancies, makegoods, billing, and approval boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SOW or IO scope, campaign materials, delivery, data use, discrepancy, makegood, billing, and client-commitment workflow
used_for: SOW or IO prerequisite validation
client-delivery and data-use boundary handling
contractual exception and makegood escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains marketing compliance outputs to evidence summaries and escalation when a request changes client commitments, delivery obligations, data use, or contractual exposure.

authoritative_sources.title: Code of Conduct
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 4A's is the U.S. advertising agency trade association; its public code defines agency conduct, confidentiality, proprietary-information handling, and ethical standards relevant to agency compliance workflow.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Agency professional conduct, client confidentiality, proprietary work, and ethical handling workflow
used_for: confidentiality and client work product handling
approved recipient and approved channel requirements
boundary between compliance support and agency-client commitments
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires client files, substantiation files, pitch work, and confidential campaign information to remain within approved engagement and tenant-security rules.

authoritative_sources.title: Best Practice Guidance: Agency Search Agreements (AKA New Business NDA's)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 4A's agency-search guidance directly addresses agency-client agreements, confidentiality, ownership of agency ideas and work, third-party access, reimbursement, and feedback expectations for agency workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Client engagement, NDA, pitch work, third-party access, ownership, and confidentiality workflow
used_for: client engagement and confidentiality prerequisite validation
pitch work and work product handling
third-party access and disclosure escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents unauthorized reuse or disclosure of client materials, agency work product, pitch materials, and compliance files.

authoritative_sources.title: Network Advertising Initiative Principles and Self-Regulatory Framework
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NAI is a recognized advertising self-regulatory body for network advertising and its framework defines privacy, choice, sensitive data, accountability, and data-governance expectations for ad delivery and measurement workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Audience-data, consent, consumer-choice, sensitive-data, measurement, and accountability workflow
used_for: audience-data and consent evidence gap handling
consumer-choice and sensitive-data escalation
privacy and approved-use refusal triggers
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires retargeting, audience segment, measurement, sensitive-data, and consent-signal questions to be escalated when tenant privacy facts or approval evidence are missing.

workflow_stages.name: Marketing compliance intake and authority prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm advertiser, brand, campaign, channel, platform, jurisdiction, product or service category, audience, client commitment, approval matrix, confidentiality constraints, systems of record, and applicable source hierarchy before work enters compliance review.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Obligation and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map FTC guidance, eCFR rules, self-regulatory frameworks, IAB/4A's terms, 4A's guidance, tenant policies, platform rules, SOW or IO terms, and client instructions to campaign-specific controls, evidence owners, review cadence, and escalation paths.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Evidence truth-set assembly
workflow_stages.description: Assemble client brief, campaign plan, media plan, content assets, claims grid, substantiation file, disclosure checklist, endorsement roster, influencer agreements, review records, email records, consent signals, platform checks, approvals, QA notes, and retention references into a traceable packet.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Review readiness and signoff-boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether visible records support a review-ready, evidence gap, source conflict, approval conflict, or blocked state without granting legal, editorial, privacy, platform-policy, contractual, or final compliance signoff.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Exception and remediation escalation
workflow_stages.description: Route missing evidence, contradictory records, policy-versus-practice conflicts, disclosure defects, endorsement gaps, review issues, email opt-out gaps, consent conflicts, platform rejections, or approval blockers to accountable owners with required remediation evidence.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Client delivery, confidentiality, and closeout evidence
workflow_stages.description: Maintain approved-recipient, approved-channel, engagement-scope, client-rights, consumer-data, substantiation-file, unreleased-creative, and retention checks before delivering review packets or marking compliance support complete.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: Tenant marketing compliance policy, service model, source hierarchy, approval matrix, confidentiality constraints, escalation thresholds, and record-retention rules
Client brief, engagement scope, SOW, IO, campaign plan, media plan, channel plan, launch timeline, target-audience notes, jurisdiction, product category, and regulated-vertical flags
Content assets, copy deck, claims grid, substantiation file, disclosure checklist, native label evidence, screenshot proof, creative QA notes, and legal or editorial review notes

Endorsement roster, influencer agreements, creator briefs, material-connection disclosures, testimonial permissions, review-source records, incentive records, moderation logs, and aggregate-rating support

Email campaign records, sender identity support, subject-line support, ad-identification review, physical address evidence, unsubscribe workflow proof, suppression-list handling, and preference-center evidence
Audience-data records, consent signals, sensitive-audience flags, retargeting or measurement setup, platform policy checks, consumer-choice evidence, and approved-use records
Project, ticket, document, proofing, legal review, platform policy, email service provider, consent management, client communication, remediation, closeout, and retention records
systems_of_record: document management and client file system
content and creative asset management system
legal, editorial, privacy, and compliance review system
platform policy review or publisher portal
email service provider and suppression-list system
consent management, preference, audience, and data-governance systems
client communication and approved delivery systems
audit log, remediation, retention, and data-security system

allowed: Determine whether documented marketing compliance evidence supports moving a work item to intake accepted, review ready, blocked for evidence, blocked for owner review, exception, remediation, escalation, complete, or closed.

Prepare evidence-backed control-obligation matrices, evidence gap summaries, review packets, exception remediation notes, policy-conflict notes, confidentiality hold notes, and adjacent-owner handoff packages.

Layer tenant policies, platform policies, SOW or IO terms, client instructions, and approval rules on top of public FTC, eCFR, IAB/4A's, 4A's, and NAI baselines while recording assumptions and source hierarchy.
escalate: The applicable jurisdiction, product category, campaign channel, platform, source hierarchy, tenant policy, client approval rule, or system record is missing and would change the control outcome.
Claims, substantiation, disclosure, endorsement, review, email, audience-data, platform, tenant, SOW, IO, client, or operational records disagree materially.
Legal interpretation, regulator-facing wording, editorial approval, privacy-law posture, targeting, audience-data use, client rights, or professional judgment determines the answer.
Policy and operational practice conflict on whether launch, clearance, delivery, disclosure placement, review use, email deployment, or closeout can proceed.

A confidentiality, approved-recipient, approved-channel, unreleased-creative, client-rights, substantiation-file, platform-credential, consumer-data, or data-security conflict affects delivery or use of information.
refuse: Requests to approve legal compliance, claims, disclosures, endorsements, testimonials, reviews, native labels, email compliance posture, privacy posture, editorial copy, platform exceptions, client commitments, makegoods, credits, contractual exceptions, or final client acceptance.

Requests to launch, clear, mark complete, or close without campaign scope, source context, substantiation, disclosure, endorsement, review, email, consent, platform, approval, remediation, communication, or retention evidence.

Requests to disclose, reuse, export, showcase, or transmit client files, consumer data, audience data, unreleased creative, substantiation files, performance data, pitch work, or platform credentials outside documented consent, engagement scope, approved channel, approved recipient, or tenant confidentiality rules.

domain_failure_modes: Treating a partial client brief, informal chat approval, stale claims grid, or old platform policy check as sufficient support for launch readiness.

Clearing campaign content without substantiation, disclosure placement evidence, endorsement roster checks, review-source records, email opt-out evidence, platform policy evidence, or required approvals.
Presenting evidence packaging as legal advice, editorial approval, privacy-law interpretation, platform-policy override, contractual signoff, or formal compliance certification.
Suppressing a disclosure, endorsement, review, opt-out, consent, platform, confidentiality, or approval exception to meet a launch date.
Using operational practice to override tenant policy, SOW or IO terms, client approval rules, FTC guidance, eCFR rules, platform policy, or self-regulatory frameworks.

Disclosing or reusing client files, consumer records, audience segments, substantiation files, unreleased creative, campaign performance data, pitch work, or platform credentials through an unapproved recipient, purpose, system, or channel.

Flattening search, social, influencer, retail media, native advertising, display, video, CTV, audio, email, PR, owned content, and experiential workflows into generic campaign tasks without channel and platform evidence context.

Treating review suppression, incentivized testimonials, material connections, native labels, email opt-outs, or consent signals as simple copy edits instead of compliance controls requiring evidence and owner review.
Confidently reconciling conflicting public guidance, tenant policy, platform rules, SOW or IO terms, client instructions, and operational practice without human owner resolution.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence
Client or content workflow handling with evidence and approval awareness

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction, product category, channel, and platform-specific requirements
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses public FTC, eCFR, IAB/4A's, 4A's, and NAI sources as the baseline. Tenant context must identify jurisdiction, product or service category, channel, platform, publisher, audience, and source hierarchy before the lane can issue more than a safe hold or evidence request.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant authority for campaign clearance or mechanical launch gating
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The specification-based baseline does not grant clearance, launch, platform mutation, email-send, or closeout authority. A runtime may only perform mechanical gating or mutation if tenant policy explicitly grants that authority and all evidence, approval, audit, and rollback prerequisites are retrieved.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Legal sufficiency, editorial judgment, privacy posture, and regulator-facing decisions
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane treats these as escalation triggers because public sources, state and sector rules, platform policies, tenant commitments, and client instructions can differ materially.

notes: The research set covers workflow, terminology, evidence, quality handling, confidentiality, regulated-content boundaries, audience-data handling, and failure modes using FTC, eCFR, IAB/4A's, 4A's, and NAI sources.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because the pack explicitly blocks or routes when jurisdiction, product category, channel, platform, client authority, SOW or IO terms, tenant policy, confidentiality, approval, legal, editorial, privacy, platform, or contractual facts are missing.
